Description: Verify password rejected at just below minimum boundary (7 chars)
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid Name, Email, and Phone
2. Enter 7-character Password
3. Click "Register"
Input Data:
Name: John
Email: sec01@test.com
Password: Valid12
Expected Result: System displays HTTP 400 with error: "Invalid password policy"
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Registration rejected
Fail: Account created

Description: Verify password accepted at exact minimum boundary (8 chars)
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid Name, Email, Phone
2. Enter 8-char Password (with 1 Uppercase & 1 Number)
3. Click "Register"
Input Data:
Name: John
Email: sec01@test.com
Password: Valid123
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 201/200 Registration successful
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Account created successfully
Fail: Registration rejected

Description: Verify password accepted at exact maximum boundary (20 chars)

3. Click "Register"
Input Data:

Email: sec01@test.com
Password: Valid12345678901234
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 201/200 Registration successful

Description: Verify password rejected when missing complexity criteria (within valid length)
Test Steps:
1. Enter password missing uppercase (10 chars)
2. Click Register
3. Enter password missing number (10 chars)
4. Click Register
Input Data:
Name: John
Email: sec01@test.com

Pass 1: validpass1 (No Uppercase)
Pass 2: Validpassw (No Number)
Expected Result: System displays HTTP 400 with error: "Invalid password policy"
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Registration rejected
Fail: Account created

Description: Verify account is locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid Email
2. Enter incorrect Password 5 times
3. Enter correct Password on the 6th attempt
Input Data:
Email: lock@test.com
Pass 1-5: WrongPass
Pass 6: CorrectPass123
Expected Result: System displays "Account locked" on the 5th attempt and rejects the 6th attempt even with correct password
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Account locked
Fail: Login proceeds or continues to allow attempts
Post-conditions: User status updated to "locked" in DB

Description: Verify API rejects requests with an expired JWT Token
Pre-conditions: User has an expired JWT token (older than 1 week)
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /reservations/{id}
2. Include expired token in Authorization header
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 401 Authentication required
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected (401)
Fail: Data returned (200)

Description: Verify SQL Injection is prevented on the Login page
Test Steps:
1. Enter SQL injection payload in Email field
2. Enter any Password
3. Click "Login"
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 400 Invalid input data or 401 Authentication required
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Login rejected
Fail: System grants access

Description: Verify system prevents XSS injection in Special Request field
Pre-conditions: User is logged in and has valid token
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /reservations
2. Fill reservation details
3. Enter XSS script in Special Request
Expected Result: System returns 400 Invalid input data OR sanitizes/encodes the script
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Input rejected or sanitized
Fail: Script saved as raw text and executed
Post-conditions: DB contains no script or safe encoded text

Description: Verify API Rate Limit prevents DoS/Spam bookings
Pre-conditions: Automation tool (e.g., JMeter) is set up. User has a valid token
Test Steps: 1. Execute script to send 100 POST requests to create reservations within 1 second using the same token
Input Data: 100 identical POST requests to /reservations in 1 sec
Expected Result: System processes initial requests within limit, then drops the rest and returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Rate limit triggers (429 returned)
Fail: Server crashes or processes all 100 requests
Post-conditions: Only the allowed limit of reservations are created in DB

Description: Verify API rejects requests missing the Authorization header
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /reservations
2. Do not include Authorization header
Input Data:
Valid reservation JSON body
No Header
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 401 Authentication required
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected (401)
Fail: Request processed

Description: Verify UI Middleware: Customer cannot access Admin/Staff pages
Test Steps:
1. Navigate directly to Admin URL via browser address bar
2. Navigate directly to Staff URL
Expected Result: System redirects the user to home/unauthorized page or displays 403 Forbidden
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Access denied and redirected
Fail: Admin/Staff page loads

Description: Verify UI Middleware: Staff cannot access Admin pages
Test Steps: 1. Navigate directly to Admin URL via browser address bar
Expected Result: System redirects the user to home/unauthorized page or displays 403 Forbidden
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Access denied and redirected
Fail: Admin page loads

Description: Verify API RBAC: Customer token cannot access Staff/Admin APIs
Pre-conditions: Customer has a valid JWT token
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to Admin API
2. Send GET request to Staff API with Customer token
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected (403)
Fail: Data returned (200)

Description: Verify API RBAC: Staff token cannot access Admin/Customer APIs
Pre-conditions: Staff has a valid JWT token
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to Admin API
2. Send POST request to Customer API with Staff token
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected (403)
Fail: Data returned (200)

Description: Verify API RBAC: Admin token cannot access Staff/Customer APIs
Pre-conditions: Admin has a valid JWT token
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to Customer API
2. Send API to manage walk-ins (Staff only) with Admin token
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected (403)
Fail: Data returned (200)

Description: Verify that user passwords are securely hashed in the database (No Plain Text)
Pre-conditions: Tester has direct access to view the Database records
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /auth/register to create a new user
2. Open Database client (e.g., DBeaver, pgAdmin)
3. Query the users table for the newly registered email
4. Inspect the password column
Input Data:
Name: Jane Doe
Email: hash@test.com
Password: SecurePass123
Expected Result: The password field in the database contains a hashed string (e.g., starting with $2b$ for bcrypt or $argon2id$) and NOT the plain text SecurePass123.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Password is hashed and unreadable
Fail: Password is saved in plain text
Post-conditions: User record exists with hashed password

Description: Verify DTO: Login API response payload does not expose the user's password
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /auth/login with valid credentials
2. Intercept and inspect the JSON response body from the server
Expected Result: The response body only contains authorized fields (e.g., token, role, userId). The password or hash field MUST NOT exist in the JSON.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Sensitive fields are excluded
Fail: Response leaks password or hash

Description: Verify DTO: Registration API response does not expose sensitive data
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /auth/register with valid new user data
2. Intercept and inspect the JSON response body
Input Data:
Name: Test DTO
Email: dto@test.com
Password: DtoPass123
Expected Result: The response body only returns success message and non-sensitive data (e.g., userId, message). The password field is excluded.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Sensitive fields are excluded
Fail: Response leaks password or hash

Description: Verify DTO: Data retrieval APIs do not leak password hashes via joined tables
Pre-conditions: User has reservations. API /admin/reservations is functioning
Test Steps:
1. Authenticate as Admin to get Token
2. Send GET request to /admin/reservations
3. Inspect the JSON array containing customer data
Expected Result: The response returns reservation data (e.g., reservationId, customerName). Joined user object data strictly excludes the password field.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: No passwords/hashes leaked in data arrays
Fail: System dumps full user schema including hashes

Description: Verify Customer A cannot view Customer B's reservation details via API
Pre-conditions:
1. Customer A has valid Token A
2. Customer B has valid Reservation ID: 5005
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to view reservation details
2. Use Customer B's reservation ID but authenticate with Customer A's token
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 404 Not Found. Information is not disclosed.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Data is protected (404)
Fail: Returns Customer B's data (HTTP 200)

Description: Verify Customer A cannot modify Customer B's reservation via API
Pre-conditions:
1. Customer A has valid Token A
2. Customer B has valid Reservation ID: 5005
Test Steps:
1. Send PUT/PATCH request to modify reservation details (e.g., change guest count)
2. Use Customer B's reservation ID but authenticate with Customer A's token
Expected Result: System returns HTTP 404 Not Found. Update is rejected.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Request rejected
Fail: Reservation successfully updated (HTTP 200/204)
Post-conditions: Customer B's reservation remains unchanged in DB

Description: Verify Customer A cannot access Customer B's reservation page via URL tampering
Pre-conditions:
1. Customer A has valid Token A
2. Customer B has valid Reservation ID: 5005
Test Steps: 1. Customer A navigates directly to the reservation details URL in the browser, changing the ID to Customer B's ID
Expected Result: System prevents access, displays "404 Not Found" page.
Pass/Fail Criteria:
Pass: Access denied or redirected gracefully
Fail: Web page loads Customer B's reservation details
